# Cookey
> Source: https://github.com/Lakr233/Cookey
> Site: https://cookey.sh

Cookey captures authenticated browser sessions from a mobile device and delivers
them encrypted to a CLI terminal as Playwright-compatible `storageState` JSON.

## Install
Download the latest binary from GitHub Releases:
  https://github.com/Lakr233/Cookey/releases/latest/download/cookey-macOS.zip

Assets:
  https://github.com/Lakr233/Cookey/releases/latest/download/cookey-macOS.zip
    macOS universal (arm64 + x86_64), signed & notarized
  https://github.com/Lakr233/Cookey/releases/latest/download/cookey-linux-x86_64.zip
    Linux x86_64
  https://github.com/Lakr233/Cookey/releases/latest/download/cookey-linux-aarch64.zip
    Linux aarch64
  https://github.com/Lakr233/Cookey/releases/latest/download/skills.md
    Detailed CLI reference and skill file for agent/tool harnesses

`llms.txt` is the concise discovery document. `skills.md` is the detailed CLI
reference and skill file for agent and tool-call workflows.

## Skill File
Install and use the skill by downloading:
  https://github.com/Lakr233/Cookey/releases/latest/download/skills.md

Reference documentation:
  https://github.com/Lakr233/Cookey/blob/main/skills.md

Suggested agent workflow:
  1. Install the `cookey` binary for the local platform.
  2. Load `skills.md` into the tool-call harness as the CLI contract and usage reference.
  3. Prefer grouped commands such as `cookey request start`, `cookey request status`, and `cookey session export`.
  4. When presenting a login request to the user, always include the CLI-provided clickable HTTPS `jump_link`, plus the pair key and CLI fingerprint as plain text.

## Commands
  cookey request start <url>
    Create a new login request, print the pair key / jump link, and start a local waiter
  cookey request refresh <url>
    Reuse the latest local session for the same target as seed state and request a refreshed session
  cookey request status [rid]
    Inspect local request state, or the latest local daemon/session summary when no rid is provided
  cookey session export [rid]
    Export a local session as Playwright `storageState` JSON
  cookey session list
    List local request/session records
  cookey session delete <rid>
    Delete one inactive local request/session pair
  cookey session clean
    Delete all inactive local request/session pairs
  cookey config get [key]
    Read configured defaults
  cookey config set <key> <value>
    Persist configured defaults

## Process Model
Default request behavior is detached:
  `cookey request start` and `cookey request refresh` launch a background daemon
  process that waits for the encrypted session and writes the local session file
  under `~/.cookey/sessions/<rid>.json`.

Foreground behavior:
  `--attach` runs the same wait/decrypt/write flow inline in the current process.

Warning:
  Unless your tool call harness supports automatic process detach (e.g., `nohup`
  or an init system), DO NOT use `--attach`. Without detach, the verification code
  will expire when the parent process exits.

## Workflow
  cookey request start https://example.com/login --qr
  # -> Pair key / fingerprint / QR appear in terminal
  # -> User scans with the Cookey app and logs in
  # -> Detached daemon waits for encrypted session delivery
  cookey request status --latest --watch
  # -> Wait until the request becomes ready
  cookey session export --latest --out storageState.json --pretty
  # -> Export Playwright-compatible storageState JSON

## Playwright Usage
  import { chromium } from "@playwright/test";
  const browser = await chromium.launch();
  const context = await browser.newContext({ storageState: "storageState.json" });
  const page = await context.newPage();
